task:
/plan The patch introduces two user-facing regressions by breaking direct execution of the dataset generator scripts from a normal checkout, and it leaves a hole in the new artifact-
  verification gate because missing proof artifacts only warn instead of failing. Those issues mean the change is not safe to merge as-is.

  Full review comments:

  - [P1] Restore repo-local import bootstrapping for generate-demo-data.py — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\generate-demo-data.py:32-32
    This script is still documented and invoked as python scripts/generate-demo-data.py from a checkout, but removing the src path bootstrap means the new top-level from
    ado_git_repo_insights... import now fails unless the package has already been installed editable. In a plain checkout, python -S scripts/generate-demo-data.py --help now raises
    ModuleNotFoundError, so local regeneration and any tooling that shells out to the script without a prior pip install -e . regress immediately.
  - [P1] Keep generate-synthetic-dataset.py runnable from a fresh checkout — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\generate-synthetic-dataset.py:18-24
    This file had the same bootstrap removed, so it now depends on ado_git_repo_insights already being installed in the interpreter. That breaks the repo’s direct-invocation workflow for
    the synthetic dataset generator: python -S scripts/generate-synthetic-dataset.py --help fails with ModuleNotFoundError. Because this script is called directly by tests/docs/tooling,
    the regression is broader than a type-only refactor.
  - [P1] Fail --verify-artifacts when a proof artifact is missing — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\check_rule_disable_invariants.py:567-570
    The new CI/preflight gate is supposed to prove why S603/S311 are globally disabled, but a deleted .rule-disable-audit-*.json only emits a warning here and still exits successfully.
    That means a PR can remove one of the committed proof artifacts and still pass che
user_feedback_text:
/plan The patch introduces two user-facing regressions by breaking direct execution of the dataset generator scripts from a normal checkout, and it leaves a hole in the new artifact-
  verification gate because missing proof artifacts only warn instead of failing. Those issues mean the change is not safe to merge as-is.

  Full review comments:

  - [P1] Restore repo-local import bootstrapping for generate-demo-data.py — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\generate-demo-data.py:32-32
    This script is still documented and invoked as python scripts/generate-demo-data.py from a checkout, but removing the src path bootstrap means the new top-level from
    ado_git_repo_insights... import now fails unless the package has already been installed editable. In a plain checkout, python -S scripts/generate-demo-data.py --help now raises
    ModuleNotFoundError, so local regeneration and any tooling that shells out to the script without a prior pip install -e . regress immediately.

task: [Request interrupted by user for tool use]

Running the new mypy src/ tests/ scripts/ command already fails on the current tree, so this change makes both CI and scripts/run_pr_preflight.py red on a clean checkout. In this
    branch, mypy reports many import-untyped errors for test imports like ado_git_repo_insights.* plus new valid-type/attr-defined errors in scripts/generate-synthetic-dataset.py, so the
    added gate cannot pass without further config or code changes.
user_feedback_text:
Running the new mypy src/ tests/ scripts/ command already fails on the current tree, so this change makes both CI and scripts/run_pr_preflight.py red on a clean checkout. In this
    branch, mypy reports many import-untyped errors for test imports like ado_git_repo_insights.* plus new valid-type/attr-defined errors in scripts/generate-synthetic-dataset.py, so the
    added gate cannot pass without further config or code changes.
